title: Listing Containers and Viewing Logs
content:
### docker ps — Listing Containers

`docker ps` lists running containers. Common variants:

```bash
docker ps              # running containers only
docker ps -a           # all containers (including stopped)
docker ps -q           # quiet mode — only container IDs
docker ps -l           # latest created container
```

### Filtering and Formatting

```bash
# Filter by status
docker ps -a --filter "status=exited"
docker ps --filter "name=web"

# Custom format
docker ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Ports}}"
docker ps --format "json"
```

### docker logs — Viewing Logs

```bash
docker logs my-container          # all logs
docker logs -f my-container       # follow (tail -f style)
docker logs --tail 100 my-container  # last 100 lines
docker logs --timestamps my-container  # with timestamps
docker logs --since 5m my-container    # last 5 minutes
```

### docker stats — Live Resource Usage

Shows real-time CPU, memory, network, and disk I/O for running containers:

```bash
docker stats
docker stats my-container
docker stats --no-stream  # single shot, no live feed
```

### docker top — Processes Inside a Container

```bash
docker top my-container
docker top my-container aux  # with aux flags
```

### docker inspect — Detailed Metadata

Returns JSON with full configuration, network settings, mount points, etc.:

```bash
docker inspect my-container
docker inspect my-container | grep IPAddress

# Extract specific fields with --format
docker inspect --format '{{.State.Status}}' my-container
docker inspect --format '{{range .NetworkSettings.Networks}}{{.IPAddress}}{{end}}' my-container
```
codeExample:
docker ps -a --filter "status=exited"
docker logs -f --tail 50 my-container
docker stats --no-stream
docker inspect --format '{{.State.Status}}' my-container
